body.text: These are some important concepts to learn in order to begin learning JavaScript.

body.desc: When defining a variable, you use the 'let' keyword followed by the variable name and an initial value
Print whatever is inside 'count' so you can see it. This is your proof that the code worked — use it after every line when you're learning.

lines: // This will print 5 to the console
// Remember to add ';' at the end of each line!

body.label: Cove says:
body.text: console.log shows you what's happening inside your code. Professional developers use it every single day. When something breaks, the first thing you do is console.log everything.

exercises.title: Declare 3 variables
tasks: Declare variable 1 called age set to your age.
Declare variable 2 called pets set to the number of pets you have.
Declare variable 3 called books set to the number of books you have.
exercises.hint: Don't forget to use 'let' and end each line with ';'

exercises.title: Display your saved information
tasks: For each variable, use console.log with a label, like this: console.log("age:", age)
Do the same for pets and books.
Run the code and check the console — you should see all 3 values printed.
exercises.hint: The label goes inside quotes, then a comma, then the variable name — no quotes on the variable.
